hero.name: Alex Sandr
hero.eyebrow: Ubud · Contact Improvisation · centers, axes, spirals · attention in action
hero.background: radial-gradient(ellipse at 75% 20%, hsl(15 65% 55% / 0.55) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse at 20% 80%, hsl(35 50% 30% / 0.65) 0%, transparent 60%), linear-gradient(180deg, hsl(22 50% 65%) 0%, hsl(28 35% 38%) 50%, hsl(20 45% 18%) 100%)
hero.welcome_md: To meet Alex Sandr's teaching is to be invited into a way of moving where the body and the mind stop arguing. *Clarity through movement, attention, and embodiment in action.* The room is held as a [Contact Improvisation](/people/contact-improv) class — the form Steve Paxton and his collaborators named in 1972 and which has since become an open-source grammar of two-or-more bodies negotiating shared gravity. Alex's path through that grammar is his own, and the language he uses to point students into it is his own.

facts.label: Practice
facts.value_md: Contact Improvisation. *The centers, axes and spirals of the body — how to organize the body in harmony with its natural intelligence; how to keep attention in action without falling apart.*

facts.label: Teaching language
facts.value_md: *How the mind influences the body, and how the body influences the mind. Contact Improvisation offers immediate feedback, revealing where we act from reality and where we act from our own ideas and projections.* Open to anyone practicing dance, somatic work, or meditation — beginners find a supportive foundation, experienced practitioners find a path toward greater mastery and clarity.

facts.label: Teaching position
facts.value_md: *Direct experience and embodied knowledge. To feel the form — I will guide you toward discovering this state for yourself.* Space-holding for a state the student finds in themselves through the contact — not performance, not transmission of choreography.

facts.label: Where he teaches
facts.value_md: [Moksa Dojo](/people/moksa-ubud) — Jl. Puskesmas, Sayan, Ubud. Sunday afternoons (the *Clarity* class). Also present at the open Monday [Ubud Contact Jam](https://www.facebook.com/groups/427928997659477/).

facts.label: First met by this body
facts.value_md: Monday May 11, 2026, at the Ubud Contact Jam. Wednesday May 13 class. Sunday May 17 class at the Moksa Dojo.

articles.body_md:
The Contact Improvisation thread already lived in this body as a [substrate practice](/people/contact-improv) — the form pointed toward by the chain [Liquid Bloom](/people/liquid-bloom) → first [Pagan Ritual](/people/pagan-ritual) → [Vali Soul Sanctuary](/people/vali-soul-sanctuary) → [Tammy Beattie](/people/tammy-beattie)'s [Ecstatic Movement Tribe](/people/ecstatic-movement-tribe). Alex carries that same grammar through Ubud rooms and a Russian-rooted teaching lineage rather than the Boulder ones.

The rhythm of the encounter has its own quiet signature: **Monday Contact Jam → Wednesday class → Sunday class at the [Moksa Dojo](/people/moksa-ubud)**. Three contacts inside seven days is the body telling us something has landed. The [Moksa Dojo](/people/moksa-ubud) is in Sayan — the same neighborhood as [Ranakami](/people/ilena-young) — so this teaching room lands in the spatial home the [Ubud cluster](https://github.com/seeker71/Coherence-Network/blob/main/docs/lineage/ubud-embodied-lineage.md) already holds with [Ilena Young](/people/ilena-young), [Vasudev Baba](/people/vasudev-baba), and [Elios](/people/elios).
articles.heading: Where the body met him — and the Ubud CI thread

articles.body_md:
- The Sunday *Clarity* class at the [Moksa Dojo](/people/moksa-ubud) on May 17, 2026 — and the Wednesday class that preceded it on May 13, 2026 after the Monday May 11, 2026 meeting at the Ubud Contact Jam.
- The practice of *attention in action without falling apart* — a language for the Contact Improvisation grammar that lands cleanly into the body's existing [substrate practice](/people/contact-improv).
- The Ubud carrier of a CI thread the body already held in its Boulder-rooted form. The form is open-source; what travels is the voice that teaches it. Alex is one such voice; the Ubud room is one such room.
articles.heading: What Alex Sandr has given the Coherence Network

metadata.title: Alex Sandr — Contact Improvisation in Ubud · clarity through movement, attention, and embodiment in action
metadata.description: A welcome to Alex Sandr — Russian-rooted Contact Improvisation teacher whose Sunday class at the Moksa Dojo in Sayan, Ubud holds the centers, axes and spirals of the body. Met through the Monday Ubud Contact Jam on May 11, 2026.

*Welcoming scaffold; Alex is invited to replace any part of it with his own words. Spelling held as "Alex Sandr" per the cell's report; full name, biographical arc, and CI lineage will land here as he chooses.*

note_from_body.body_md: Welcoming scaffold authored after six days of lived encounter, using Alex's own teaching language from the Sunday class flyer rather than narrating about him. His Russian roots are named; the longer biographical arc and lineage of his Contact Improvisation training are not yet attested in this body and stay open. Alex is invited to replace any part of this page with his own words.
